_comment_action: webhook_to_hive_mind (retired WS-B): the x-elastic-signature header was a
literal placeholder string ('KIBANA_HMAC_SIGNATURE'), never a computed
every delivery on this path as an invalid signature. It also targeted
127.0.0.1:8000, which only resolves to the broker from inside the
broker's own container/host, not from Kibana/Watcher's network context.
The broker now exposes a signed /webhook/dispatch endpoint (app.py) fed
by the Logstash Category 5 HTTP output instead. Restoring this watcher's
dispatch would require a real HMAC computation Watcher's webhook action
cannot produce natively (painless has no built-in HMAC-SHA256) plus a
reachable host/port — out of scope here. Left as detection-only so the
bruteforce_success signal stays visible in Kibana's Watcher history.

logging.text: SSH BRUTEFORCE SUCCESS: attacker_ip={{ctx.payload.hits.hits.0._source.source.ip}}. Dispatch to hive-mind-broker is not wired (see _comment_action) — this is detection-only; a human must action it via Kibana.
